methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Solideon is a pre-seed robotics and manufacturing company founded by Oluseun Taiwo that has raised $6.5M to date. The broader sector includes 1 public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ) and 8 private peers, among them 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. However, the private peer set lacks sufficient depth at the pre-seed stage or comparable valuation tier to support reliable median-based triangulation.

Given data constraints, the stage-progression method is the sole applicable framework. This approach applies a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from pre-seed to the subsequent stage, using the $6.5M total raised as a valuation proxy. This yields an implied valuation of $16M. No peer-median calculation was performed due to absence of three or more same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier comparables within the robotics and manufacturing universe.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $14M to $19M, centered at $16M. The primary uncertainty is the absence of narrow-set peer validation; stage-progression relies on the assumption that Solideon's trajectory aligns with sector cohort step-ups, which remains unverified given limited pre-seed robotics data. A full market-based valuation would require either identification of additional pre-seed robotics peers or direct investor-round benchmarking.
